text:
WHOLE BODY FDG PET-CT WITH CONCURRENT MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

INDICATION: 56-year-old female with history of BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma (Stage IV M1c), initially presenting with hepatic, nodal, and cutaneous metastases. Prior treatment includes dabrafenib/trametinib (discontinued at 8 months for acquired resistance) followed by ipilimumab/nivolumab for 3 cycles, complicated by Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis. Currently on nivolumab maintenance (480 mg IV every 4 weeks), approximately 23 months into PD-1 monotherapy. Comparison available to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain. This examination requested to evaluate for complete metabolic response to guide potential treatment discontinuation decision.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole body FDG PET-CT: Patient fasted for 6 hours prior to FDG administration. Blood glucose at time of injection: 94 mg/dL. 10.2 mCi F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose administered intravenously. Uptake period: 62 minutes. PET acquisition from vertex to mid-thigh performed with low-dose attenuation correction CT (without IV contrast). CT images acquired from vertex to mid-thigh during quiet breathing. PET images reconstructed using iterative algorithm with time-of-flight correction.

MRI Brain: 1.5 Tesla MRI, multiplanar sequences including T1 pre- and post-gadolinium (0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol IV), T2, FLAIR, and DWI. No significant motion artifact.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK / CERVICAL NODAL STATIONS:
No FDG-avid lesions identified in the head and neck. The right level II cervical lymph node previously enlarged to 2.2 cm at time of progression (and subsequently shrinking on serial imaging) is no longer visualized as a discrete node. Scattered small bilateral cervical and supraclavicular lymph nodes within the range of normal morphology and without FDG avidity (maximum SUV background level). No new cervical, supraclavicular, or submental FDG-avid adenopathy.

THORAX AND AXILLARY REGIONS:
Lungs: No pulmonary nodules, masses, or parenchymal infiltrates identified. No FDG-avid pulmonary lesions. Lung bases clear bilaterally. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax. No evidence of immune-related pneumonitis — lung parenchyma homogeneous without ground-glass opacity or consolidation.
Mediastinum: Mediastinal contour unremarkable. Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar FDG-avid adenopathy.
Axillary regions: The right axillary region, previously harboring bulky adenopathy (4.2 cm at initial staging, down to 0.5-0.6 cm on recent cross-sectional imaging), demonstrates no FDG-avid adenopathy. Small right axillary lymph nodes measuring up to 0.5 cm in short axis, with SUVmax at background levels (less than blood pool activity, approximately 1.4). No left axillary FDG-avid nodes. This represents complete metabolic resolution from prior right axillary disease burden.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. Two small hypodense foci are again noted in the right hepatic lobe on the low-dose CT component of the PET study: one measuring approximately 0.3 cm in hepatic segment 6 (a site of prior metastatic disease with serial decline in size and activity), and a second subcentimeter hypodensity in segment 5 measuring approximately 0.2 cm. Critically, neither of these foci demonstrates FDG uptake above background liver parenchymal activity. The SUVmax over the segment 6 focus measures 1.8, which is below or at background liver parenchymal activity (liver SUVmean 2.1). This is well below the threshold considered metabolically active. The appearance is most consistent with residual fibrotic scar or treated metastatic deposits without viable tumor. No new hepatic lesions identified.
Gallbladder: Normal, no wall thickening or calculi.
Spleen: Mildly enlarged at 13.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension; this has been stable on prior examinations and likely reflects prior immune checkpoint therapy-related splenic enlargement, a recognized benign phenomenon. No focal splenic FDG-avid lesions.
Pancreas: Normal morphology, no focal lesions, no ductal dilation.
Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands within normal size and morphology. No adrenal masses or FDG-avid lesions. No evidence of adrenal metastasis or immune-related adrenal insufficiency on imaging.
Kidneys: Normal size and echogenic appearance bilaterally. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. No FDG-avid renal lesions. Renal cortical attenuation normal.
Gastrointestinal tract: No bowel wall thickening or FDG-avid bowel segments to suggest active immune-mediated colitis. The colon wall appears normal in thickness throughout without the previously noted mild diffuse thickening seen on imaging during active colitis phase — consistent with clinical and endoscopic resolution. No peritoneal FDG-avid lesions. The previously identified peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament (0.9 cm at time of progression, subsequently resolving on cross-sectional imaging) is not visualized and there is no corresponding FDG-avid focus in that location.
Pelvis: No pelvic FDG-avid adenopathy. No pelvic masses. Inguinal lymph nodes: small bilateral inguinal lymph nodes without FDG avidity, within normal morphologic limits (all less than 0.8 cm short axis). Uterus and adnexa appear unremarkable.

SOFT TISSUES AND MUSCULOSKELETAL:
No FDG-avid subcutaneous or cutaneous nodules identified anywhere in the imaged field. Previously described subcutaneous nodules over the trunk and bilateral upper extremities — some of which were as large as 3.1 cm at initial staging — are not visualized. No new soft tissue masses. No FDG-avid osseous lesions. No evidence of skeletal metastasis. Spine: No destructive lesions or vertebral body collapse.

POST-PROCESSING / QUANTIFICATION:
Total FDG dose administered: 10.2 mCi. Blood glucose at injection: 94 mg/dL. Image quality: good. Liver SUVmean (reference): 2.1. Mediastinal blood pool SUVmean (reference): 1.6. No target lesions identified with SUVmax exceeding background levels in any compartment.

MRI BRAIN (WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM):
Brain parenchyma: No restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction. No parenchymal signal abnormality on T2/FLAIR to suggest edema, encephalomalacia, or infiltrative process. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No pachymeningeal enhancement. No parenchymal enhancing lesions identified following gadolinium administration. Specifically, no enhancing nodules, ring-enhancing masses, or lesions suspicious for intracranial metastasis are identified in the cerebral hemispheres, cerebellum, brainstem, or along the calvarium. The ventricles are normal in size and configuration. No midline shift. No hydrocephalus. Posterior fossa: no cranial nerve enhancement or cerebellar metastasis. Pituitary gland: mildly prominent in appearance but without discrete lesion or abnormal enhancement; in the setting of prior immune checkpoint therapy and known grade 2 hypothyroidism managed with levothyroxine, this appearance should be noted and can be followed, though may reflect prior immunotherapy-related hypophysitis without current activity.

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid disease identified anywhere in the body. This represents a complete metabolic response compared to the widespread disease burden documented at initial staging (bilateral axillary and inguinal adenopathy, multiple hepatic lesions, subcutaneous nodules, peritoneal implant) and at the time of progression on BRAF/MEK inhibition.

2. Two small residual hypodense hepatic foci (0.3 cm segment 6, 0.2 cm segment 5) with no FDG activity above background liver parenchyma. These are metabolically quiescent and morphologically consistent with treated deposits/fibrotic scar; no imaging features to suggest viable tumor.

3. No intracranial metastatic disease on gadolinium-enhanced MRI brain. No leptomeningeal disease.

4. No FDG-avid nodal disease in any nodal station — cervical, axillary, mediastinal, abdominal, pelvic, or inguinal. All previously involved sites show complete metabolic resolution.

5. Mildly prominent pituitary gland noted; correlation with hormonal studies recommended in the context of prior immune checkpoint therapy, though no definitive structural abnormality identified.

6. Mild stable splenomegaly without FDG-avid lesions, consistent with prior checkpoint immunotherapy exposure.

7. No imaging evidence of active immune-related pneumonitis or active immune-mediated colitis.

Overall, the findings indicate a thorough and durable response to treatment, with no residual metabolically active disease identified on whole-body FDG imaging or intracranial imaging.
